how_verified: Verified in ISOLATION (no whole-server import). Throwaway FastMCP + a real Suite(FsStore, NodeRegistry) constructed from the live store; called runsmod.register(mcp, suite); confirmed the `runs` tool registers (asyncio.run(mcp.list_tools()) -> ['runs'], schema params [limit, op, role, run_kind, run_op, since], op required). Fired a sample of each op against the live op.run log: op='list' returned {op, runs, total_records=35} with rows {address, op, run_op, turn_id, role, duration_ms, seq, ts, finish_reason}; op='find' with role='ground' returned the same shape role-filtered (all rows role==ground, correct); run_kind='cognition.run_role' correctly narrowed to run_role rows only. Teaching errors confirmed: bad selector op='results' -> corpus.py-style {error: ...}; bad run_kind='cognition.bogus' -> Suite's live-registry ValueError propagates ('the run index covers [...]'). No model / company gpu needed (read-only over the op.run event log).
notes: DELIBERATELY FOLDED ONLY list_runs + find_runs. They are the SAME resource (the #54 run index): both are a read-time projection over the op.run event log keyed by run:// addresses, scoped to the closed suite.ENGINE_RUN_OPS, both return {runs, total_records}; find_runs literally calls list_runs + a role filter. So one tool with op=list|find, no parallel path. DID NOT fold get_results / get_state / get_events into runs.py (honoured the don't-god-tool / distinct-nouns rule, which the lane spec and the audit row both explicitly license -- 'be honest if state/events do not belong'): (1) get_state(graph) and get_results(graph) are keyed by `graph_id` (a GRAPH noun, NOT a run) and results() is literally a projection of state() -- a different resource (a graph), they belong in a separate `graph`/`read` resource file. The lane spec wrote `op='results' -> get_results(address)` but get_results actually takes `graph`, not `address` -- the misnamed param is itself the evidence these were never run-scoped, and dropping them makes the proposed `address=''` param vestigial (confirmation the split is right). (2) get_events(limit) is the GLOBAL captured trajectory with NO run/graph key at all -- a different resource (the trajectory log). RECOMMENDATION for the audit's 5->~2 to complete: land get_state/get_results under a `graph` (or `read`) resource file and get_events under the same or a trajectory resource, in a SEPARATE follow-up lane -- do NOT pull them into runs.py to compensate (one file = one resource per the tools/__init__.py model). Registry-is-truth: `run_kind` maps to the engine `op` filter and is validated against the LIVE suite.ENGINE_RUN_OPS by the Suite (teaching ValueError), never a hardcoded enum here. Kept `since` (event-seq cursor) for pagination parity. The flat tools used `op` for the engine-run-op and `run_op` for the verb; the consolidated tool renames the selector to `op`=list|find and exposes the engine-run-op filter as `run_kind` to avoid the collision (run_op kept for the verb). I did NOT edit server.py -- the flat-tool removal (list_runs, find_runs) is the serial pass after this lane.
